title: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure and American Binary: Post-quantum threats require quantum-resistant solutions
why:
- Quantum computers are expected to break public-key cryptographic algorithms much more efficiently than classical computers, putting existing encrypted data at risk from the "Harvest Now, Decrypt Later" (HNDL) threat.
- The US Department of War has directed military components to prioritize the adoption of post-quantum cryptography (PQC) and phase out certain legacy protocols by 2030.
- Commercial National Security Algorithm Suite 2.0 (CNSA 2.0) mandates that national security systems, including VPNs, must support PQC beginning in January 2026, and vulnerable older algorithms must be replaced.
- American Binary’s Ambit Client VPN uses NIST Level 5 cryptography compliant with CNSA 2.0 and federal standards (FIPS 140-3 and 203) to secure data against quantum threats.
- Ambit Client is available for deployment on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure and supports secure, private, post-quantum connections for government, defense, and multiple commercial sectors.
